raw_response:
I agree with the consensus that the ANB angle in a skeletal Class III malocclusion should be negative due to the characteristic retrognathic maxilla. An additional nuance worth noting is that severe cases might even show angles slightly below -10 degrees, though typically values around -8 to -15° would still fall under classifying conditions as Class III. Nevertheless, considering standard ranges and typical presentations, option A (-10) remains appropriately representative of what would generally be observed clinically.

Therefore, my answer choice is: A.```
